enriched_points.expanded_content: Plasmonic nanostructures enhance imaging and sensing capabilities, enabling early detection and accurate diagnosis of diseases, particularly cancer, through advanced photonic interactions.
These nanostructures facilitate real-time monitoring and identification of biomarkers, significantly reducing detection times while improving sensitivity, crucial for timely clinical interventions.
Integrating plasmonics with therapeutic modalities allows tailored treatments based on individual patient profiles, optimizing efficacy and minimizing adverse effects in precision medicine.
Plasmonic nanomaterials are utilized for detecting pollutants and pathogens in environmental samples, providing rapid and sensitive methods for monitoring public health and safety.
